mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE crosses above 0.5pp threshold in 3 months
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses the threshold, or the signal out-of-sample accuracy drops below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and business demand expansion, which generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal fails to predict positive 20-day returns in over 60% of historical occurrences, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days when the economy operates above 95% of its historical peak.
failure_condition: The signal loses predictive power when INDPRO exceeds 95% of its historical maximum, with 5-day forward returns showing a negative or zero correlation during high-capacity periods.

mechanism_narrative: Steady month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates robust employment expansion, which supports corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no statistically significant positive correlation with 20-day forward returns over a 5-year rolling window, or the signal directional accuracy falls below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation reading exceeding 0.4 percent triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns remain positive in more than 65% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds the threshold, or the inflation signal fails to predict return direction with p < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly GDP growth above 2 percent and a federal funds rate below 0.5 percent creates a favorable growth-and-liquidity environment that generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark over 60-day horizons, or the interaction effect reverses direction when FEDFUNDS rises above 1.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in industrial production and a rise in the unemployment rate signals a broad economic contraction, which forces equity markets to price in lower earnings and yields negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The dual negative signal produces positive 20-day returns in over 55% of historical instances, or the predictive accuracy drops below chance levels during periods of stable macro conditions.
